item.title: Dudley Backs Powell Staying On After ‘Merciless’ Attacks on Fed
item.description: Jerome Powell’s decision to stay on as a Federal Reserve governor after his term as chair will help reassure Wall Street and the public after President Donald Trump’s pressure campaign to lower rates, according to Bill Dudley.

item.title: US Allies Look to Europe to Build a Post-Trump Global Order
item.description: Some of the US’s closest allies including Canada and the UK are warning that the international system of security and trade championed by western powers for more than 70 years is broken beyond repair — and that it’s up to Europe to fashion a new global order.

item.title: Trump-Linked World Liberty Sues Justin Sun for Defamation
item.description: World Liberty Financial said it is suing Justin Sun for defamation, accusing the billionaire crypto entrepreneur of running a coordinated media campaign against the Trump-linked project after he filed his own lawsuit against it last month.

item.title: Goldman, Blackstone Partner With Anthropic on AI Services Firm
item.description: Anthropic PBC is partnering with Blackstone Inc., Hellman & Friedman and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to form a new company that will bring artificial intelligence to a broad group of midsize companies.

item.title: South African Farms Face El Niño Drought Risk on Top of Iran War
item.description: South African farmers pinched by mounting costs from the Iran war now face the added risk of an El Niño-induced drought, with implications for lower agricultural output and higher food prices.

item.title: Standard Chartered to Sell East African Headquarters as Footprint Shrinks
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc is selling its East African head office as the lender shrinks its physical presence on the continent.

item.title: Carlyle Seeds Buyout Fund With $5 Billion in Novel Credit Deal
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. arranged a first-of-its-kind financing that will commit more than $5 billion to seed its next flagship buyout fund while repaying investors in some of its older vintages, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Chile Economic Activity Sees Monthly Rise in End to Weak Quarter
item.description: Chile’s economic activity posted the first monthly gain this year in March, according to a report published days after central bankers warned of a more adverse impact from the Middle East war on both global growth and inflation.

item.title: Banks Kick Off Sale of Junk Bonds Backing BASF Coatings Deal
item.description: Banks have kicked off the sale of roughly €1.95 billion-equivalent ($2.28 billion) of junk bonds to help finance BASF SE’s auto paint and finishing division acquisition by Carlyle Group Inc. and partner Qatar Investment Authority.

item.title: Updates From Milken Institute Global Conference
item.description: Attendees wait for a presentation during the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills. Photographer: Patrick T. Fallon/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: Treasuries Start Week Under Pressure as Oil Prices Resume Climb
item.description: Treasuries fell as heightened tensions in the Middle East kept oil prices elevated, fanning concerns over the inflation outlook.

item.title: ADB Urges Countries to Limit Fuel Subsidies to Vulnerable Groups
item.description: Japan and other economies should limit fuel subsidies to those most in need, the Asian Development Bank’s chief economist said, as prolonged Middle East tensions strain public finances.

item.title: OpenAI Finalizes $10 Billion Joint Venture With PE Firms to Deploy AI
item.description: OpenAI has raised more than $4 billion for a new joint venture that will focus on helping businesses adopt its artificial intelligence software, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chile Investors Split Over Iran War Impact as Pressures Mount
item.description: As the Middle East conflict drags on into its third month and Chile’s central bank warns that uncertainty is hitting new highs, investors are split over the impact on the local fixed-income market.

item.title: South Africa Gasoline Price Jumps to Highest in Four Years
item.description: South African gasoline prices will rise to a near four-year high and wholesale diesel cost will top 30 rand ($1.79) a liter for the first time, as government interventions failed to fully offset the impact of higher oil prices and a weaker currency tied to the Iran war.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 4, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.2% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York. The US denied a report by Iranian media that the country had struck an American naval vessel with missiles, according to a senior official.

item.title: Whatnot Has Americans Hooked on Livestream Shopping
item.description:
The app’s rapid-fire auctions are inspiring customers to spend big,
for better or worse.

item.title: Permian Landowner EagleRock Seeks to Raise $346 Million in IPO
item.description: EagleRock Land LLC, a Permian Basin landowner and management company, is seeking to raise as much as $346 million in a US initial public offering.

item.title: ‘Ultimate Dip-Buyers’ of Stocks Remain Strong as Buybacks Surge
item.description: As investors begin to wonder if a torrid rise in US equities is running out of steam, one of the market’s most important buyers shows its unwavering support remains.

item.title: US Oil Sanctions Push Beijing to Test Washington’s Limits
item.description: Beijing has told its companies to ignore American sanctions on a major oil refiner, complicating the leaders’ summit next week.

item.title: Palantir Looks to Prove It Doesn’t Belong in Software Selloff
item.description: Palantir Technologies Inc. heads into its first-quarter earnings with something to prove to investors: That it’s been wrongly caught up in the selloff that has gripped software stocks for months.

item.title: Blackstone Data Center REIT Seeks to Raise $1.75 Billion in IPO
item.description: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $1.75 billion in a US initial public offering, offering investors the chance to capitalize on a surge in building artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: How US and China’s Allies Struggle to Balance Trade Ties With Both
item.description: Big economies managing ties with the US and China are struggling to withstand shocks from both

item.title: Musk Sketches Apocalyptic Vision in Bid to Block OpenAI’s Transition
item.description: Warnings about various risks to humankind are a typical part of the billionaire’s rhetoric

item.title: US Stock Futures Dip on Reports of Increasing Mideast Tension
item.description: US stock futures saw volatile early Monday trading with news reports of Iranian missiles hitting a US Navy ship. While those reports cited IRGC-aligned Fars News Agency, a senior US official said that a ship was not hit by Iranian missiles, according to Axios, which helped futures erase some of the earlier losses.

item.title: Asia May Be Entering an AI-Fueled Industrial Super Cycle
item.description: Morgan Stanley economists see AI, the energy transition and defense spending fueling a capital expenditure-led expansion for Asia.

item.title: Amex GBT Said to Near Sale to General Catalyst-, Alpha Wave-Backed Long Lake
item.description: A startup backed by General Catalyst Partners is in advanced talks to acquire Global Business Travel Group Inc., the travel platform spun out of American Express Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Geothermal Energy Developer Fervo Seeks $1.33 Billion in US IPO
item.description: Fervo Energy Co., a geothermal energy developer, is looking to raise as much as $1.33 billion in a US initial public offering, as more firms seek to capitalize on the growing power demand for data centers.

item.title: Mexico’s Banks Handed Out Millions of Cards That Nobody Wants
item.description: The country’s die-hard cash culture is holding up against a relentless, hundred-million dollar effort from fintech companies

item.title: Immune System Biotech Odyssey Seeks $238.3 Million in US IPO
item.description: Odyssey Therapeutics Inc., a clinical stage biotechnology firm focused on autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, is seeking to raise $238.3 million in an initial public offering.
